user_message: Need help with Iron Creek Mining -- generate the watchlist. This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 17:11 EST
Subject: Re: forecast prep Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 14:26 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining forecast prep

Just checking in on the forecast prep proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 13:20 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining forecast prep

There's been a change in our timeline for forecast prep. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-03-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is forecast prep.

[00:07:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:09:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:19:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:24:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
